historical_cases.name: Humira / Anti-TNF Biologics Supercycle (2003–2012)
historical_cases.peak_move: AbbVie predecessor and biologic-focused firms observed multi-hundred-percent cumulative gains across the cycle; sector re-rated from niche to dominant pharma category
historical_cases.exit_trigger: Biosimilar entry, patent cliffs, and payer pushback on pricing eventually compressed multiples; no single exit event but gradual margin pressure over 2012–2016

historical_cases.name: PCSK9 Inhibitor Launch Euphoria (2015–2017)
historical_cases.peak_move: Regeneron and Amgen observed sharp valuation surges ahead of FDA approval; peak enthusiasm faded materially within roughly two years of launch
historical_cases.exit_trigger: Payer coverage restrictions, high list prices versus demonstrated real-world adherence, and disappointing initial uptake curves triggered a significant de-rating despite clinical efficacy

historical_cases.name: Ozempic / Semaglutide Weight-Loss Re-Label Wave (2021–2023)
historical_cases.peak_move: Novo Nordisk observed peak market cap surpassing $500B, briefly becoming Europe's most valuable company; broad GLP-1 basket re-rated dramatically; obesity indication expanded thematic to Wegovy approval 2021
historical_cases.exit_trigger: Supply shortages temporarily capped commercial momentum; valuation compression began late 2023 as competitive pipeline disclosures from Eli Lilly and others reset relative positioning

historical_cases.name: Eli Lilly Tirzepatide / Mounjaro-to-Zepbound Approval Cycle (2022–2024)
historical_cases.peak_move: Lilly observed sustained multi-year re-rating, with shares roughly tripling from pre-tirzepatide baseline to 2024 peak; demonstrated superior weight-loss efficacy data extended investor enthusiasm beyond typical approval windows
historical_cases.exit_trigger: Cycle matured as consensus estimates became crowded, manufacturing scale questions persisted, and broader GLP-1 basket saw rotation as next-generation oral candidates (orforglipron, danuglipron) began shifting investor attention

differences.factor: Addressable market scope has expanded structurally beyond obesity and T2D to include cardiovascular outcomes (SELECT trial), sleep apnea, kidney disease, alcohol use disorder, and potentially neurodegeneration — each representing an independent multi-billion-dollar indication

Manufacturing capacity constraints (peptide synthesis, fill-finish) observed through 2023–2025 have catalyzed unprecedented capex commitments by Novo and Lilly, but oral small-molecule GLP-1 agonists may dramatically lower production barriers if approved, potentially compressing incumbent moats faster than prior biologic cycles

U.S. Medicare coverage for anti-obesity medications remains contested but directionally expanding; CMS pilot programs and potential legislative action in 2025–2026 could represent a step-change demand unlock with no historical analog in prior drug class cycles

Next-generation dual/triple agonists (GIP/GLP-1/glucagon), oral formulations, and combination therapies with muscle-preserving agents represent a pipeline depth not observed in prior single-mechanism drug class cycles, suggesting a multi-wave innovation cadence rather than a single approval event

similarities: Payer pushback on list price and coverage restrictions historically follows initial launch enthusiasm in high-cost specialty drug categories, and early signals of this pattern may already be observed in GLP-1 prior authorization data

Competitive pipeline disclosure has historically compressed first-mover multiples well before new entrants reach market — consistent with the pattern observed as oral candidates advanced through Phase 2/3 trials in 2024–2025
Supply shortages creating a gap between clinical demand and commercial revenue are a recurring feature of biologics launches, historically resolved over 18–36 months as manufacturing scales
this_time_different.observation: The GLP-1 cycle may be structurally longer than prior drug class themes due to multi-indication pipeline breadth and potential policy-driven demand unlocks, but it remains subject to the same historical compression forces of pricing pressure and competitive entry that eventually de-rated analogous drug class supercycles.

exit_signals: Sustained deceleration in prescription volume growth (TRx data) for leading GLP-1 agents over two or more consecutive quarters, suggesting market penetration is plateauing relative to consensus expectations
FDA approval or positive Phase 3 readout for an oral small-molecule GLP-1 agonist from a major competitor, materially disrupting injectable franchise moat assumptions
Major payer or CMS policy decision limiting coverage or imposing mandatory rebate structures that visibly compress net pricing below consensus models

Clinical trial failure or unexpected safety signal (e.g., muscle mass loss, thyroid findings, GI complications at scale) in a pivotal indication expansion study, triggering broad class-level re-assessment

Consensus sell-side EPS estimates for lead GLP-1 franchises begin being revised downward for two or more future years simultaneously, signaling that the growth premium in valuations is no longer supported by forward earnings trajectory
duration_type_reasoning: GLP-1 obesity drug adoption represents a multi-year structural transformation of the pharmaceutical market driven by expanding indications, manufacturing buildout, and potential policy-driven coverage changes, with no single bounded endpoint event.

real_world_timeline.description: The modern GLP-1 obesity investment supercycle is observed to have originated with the FDA approval of semaglutide (Wegovy) for chronic weight management in June 2021, which reframed obesity as a treatable chronic disease and catalyzed broad sector re-rating.
